# services.librechat

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.librechat`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.librechat.credentials` | `attribute set of absolute path` | Environment variables which are loaded from the contents of files at a file paths, mainly used for secrets. See [LibreChat environment variables](https://www.librechat.ai/docs/configuration/dotenv). Alternatively you can use `services.librechat.credentialsFile` to define all the variables in a single file. |
| `services.librechat.credentialsFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to a file that contains environment variables. See [LibreChat environment variables](https://www.librechat.ai/docs/configuration/dotenv). Example content of the file: `CREDS_KEY=6d6deb03cdfb27ea454f6b9ddd42494bdce4af25d50d8aee454ddce583690cc5` Alternatively you can use `services.librechat.credentials` to define the value of each variable in a separate file. |
| `services.librechat.dataDir` | `absolute path` | Absolute path for where the LibreChat server will use as its data directory to store logs, user uploads, and generated images. |
| `services.librechat.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the LibreChat server. |
| `services.librechat.enableLocalDB` | `boolean` | Whether to enable a local mongodb instance. |
| `services.librechat.env` | `open submodule of attribute set of (string or absolute path or (string or signed integer convertible to it) or (string or floating point number convertible to it) or (string or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive) convertible to it) or (string or boolean convertible to it))` | Environment variables that will be set for the service. See [LibreChat environment variables](https://www.librechat.ai/docs/configuration/dotenv). |
| `services.librechat.env.LIBRECHAT_LOG_DIR` | `string` | Logs will be saved into this directory. By default it is relative to `services.librechat.dataDir`. |
| `services.librechat.env.PORT` | `string or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive) convertible to it` | The value that will be passed to the PORT environment variable, telling LibreChat what to listen on. |
| `services.librechat.group` | `string` | The group to run the service as. |
| `services.librechat.meilisearch` | `submodule` | See [LibreChat search feature](https://www.librechat.ai/docs/features/search). |
| `services.librechat.meilisearch.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable and configure Meilisearch locally for Librechat. You will manually need to set `services.meilisearch.masterKeyFile`. |
| `services.librechat.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Whether to open the port in the firewall. |
| `services.librechat.package` | `package` | The librechat package to use. |
| `services.librechat.settings` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | A free-form attribute set that will be written to librechat.yaml. See the [LibreChat configuration options](https://www.librechat.ai/docs/configuration/librechat_yaml). You can use environment variables by wrapping them in ${}. Take care to escape the $ character. |
| `services.librechat.user` | `string` | The user to run the service as. |
